evals.prompt:
Classify soils from the following borehole log data from a road project on the Kampala-Jinja Highway in Uganda. The data is from Test Pit TP-08 at chainage 15+200.

Depth 0.0-0.8 m: Reddish-brown gravelly soil. Lab results: LL = 35%, PL = 18%, PI = 17%. Grading: 25% passing 0.075 mm, 50% passing 2.0 mm, 65% passing 4.75 mm.

Depth 0.8-1.5 m: Reddish-brown clayey soil with some gravel. Lab results: LL = 44%, PL = 20%, PI = 24%. Grading: 40% passing 0.075 mm, 62% passing 2.0 mm, 78% passing 4.75 mm.

Depth 1.5-2.0 m: Dark brown to grey clay, very sticky. Lab results: LL = 58%, PL = 22%, PI = 36%. Grading: 55% passing 0.075 mm, 75% passing 2.0 mm, 88% passing 4.75 mm.

Classify each layer using AASHTO system, calculate Group Index, provide USCS classification, and assess subgrade suitability for pavement design.
evals.expected_output: Layer 1 (0.0-0.8 m): AASHTO A-2-6, GI approximately 0-2. USCS: SC (clayey sand). Good laterite, suitable for subgrade (likely S4-S5 range with CBR 8-20%). Layer 2 (0.8-1.5 m): AASHTO A-2-7 or A-7-5, GI approximately 3-6. USCS: CL or SC. Moderate quality, marginal subgrade. Layer 3 (1.5-2.0 m): AASHTO A-7-6 (since LL-30=28 < PI=36), GI approximately 10-15. USCS: CH. Very poor subgrade -- potentially expansive clay. Escalation flag for expansive soil below 1.5 m requiring treatment or replacement.

assertions.text: Correctly classifies the upper layer (0.0-0.8 m) as AASHTO A-2-6 with GI in the 0-2 range
Correctly identifies the lowest layer (1.5-2.0 m) as AASHTO A-7-6 (not A-7-5), noting that PI > LL-30 indicates expansive clay
Calculates Group Index values that are reasonable for each layer (increasing with depth as soil quality decreases)
Provides engineering assessment of subgrade suitability, identifying the deepest layer as problematic (expansive, very poor subgrade)
Raises an escalation or warning flag for the expansive clay at depth, recommending swell testing or subgrade treatment

evals.prompt:
Classify soils from a trial pit on a highway scheme near Oxford, United Kingdom. The geology is Oxford Clay Formation.

Trial Pit TP-03, depth 0.0-3.0 m:

Depth 0.0-0.3 m: Dark brown topsoil with roots.
Depth 0.3-1.5 m: Firm brown slightly silty CLAY. Lab results: LL = 48%, PL = 21%, PI = 27%. Percentage passing 63 microns = 72%.
Depth 1.5-3.0 m: Stiff dark grey CLAY with occasional shell fragments. Lab results: LL = 62%, PL = 25%, PI = 37%. Percentage passing 63 microns = 85%.

Provide a BS 5930 soil description and classify using BS EN ISO 14688. Assess subgrade suitability for highway design and estimate equilibrium CBR.
evals.expected_output: BS 5930 descriptions should follow the standard format. Stratum 1 (0.3-1.5 m): Firm brown slightly silty CLAY, high plasticity, Oxford Clay Formation. PI = 27, equilibrium CBR approximately 2-4%. Stratum 2 (1.5-3.0 m): Stiff dark grey CLAY, very high plasticity, Oxford Clay Formation. PI = 37, equilibrium CBR approximately 1.5-3%. Both layers are poor subgrade material requiring capping. London/Oxford Clay shrink-swell should be noted.

assertions.text: Provides BS 5930 format soil descriptions (consistency, colour, principal soil type) for each stratum
Classifies soils using BS EN ISO 14688 or notes the high plasticity classification (PI > 25)
Estimates equilibrium CBR values in the 1-5% range for both clay strata, appropriate for high-plasticity clay
Identifies both layers as poor subgrade material requiring capping or stabilisation for highway design

evals.prompt:
Classify soils from a test pit on a district road project in Gulu, northern Uganda. Only visual field descriptions are available -- no laboratory test results have been obtained.

Test Pit TP-05, depth 0.0-1.5 m:

Depth 0.0-0.5 m: Reddish-brown gravelly material, crumbly when dry. Moderate amount of fines. Some quartz pebbles visible.
Depth 0.5-1.2 m: Reddish-brown clayey material, moderately plastic, can be rolled into a thread but crumbles at about 5 mm diameter. Sticky when wet.
Depth 1.2-1.5 m: Yellowish-brown sandy material with very little fines. Non-plastic.

Classify these soils based on visual description only and recommend what laboratory tests are needed.
evals.expected_output: Visual classification should be attempted for all three layers with clear caveats. Layer 1: Probable laterite gravel, tentatively A-2 group. Layer 2: Lateritic clay, tentatively A-6 or A-7. Layer 3: Sand, tentatively A-3 or A-2-4. All classifications must be flagged as preliminary/visual only. Must recommend laboratory testing: Atterberg limits, grading analysis, CBR (soaked, 4-day), and compaction test for all three layers. Escalation flag for no lab data -- classification is unreliable without testing.

assertions.text: Attempts visual classification for all three layers, assigning tentative AASHTO groups with clear uncertainty flags
Flags that classification without laboratory data is preliminary and unreliable, raising an escalation or warning
Recommends specific laboratory tests needed (Atterberg limits, grading analysis at minimum) to confirm classification
